name: Chocolate and Vanilla Chip Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Spray one large or two small cookie sheets with nonstick cooking spray.
3. Sift the flour mixture: Lightly spoon the flour into a measuring cup and level off. In a medium bowl, combine the sifted flour, cocoa powder, and baking powder; mix well.
4. Combine sugar and butter: In a large bowl, whisk together the granulated sugar and brown sugar with the softened butter or margarine until well blended.
5. Add eggs: Beat in the eggs one at a time until smooth.
6. Incorporate vanilla chips: Stir in the white vanilla chips.
7. Form dough into rolls: Using spray-coated hands, firmly shape the dough into three equal rolls, about 7 inches long.
8. Place rolls on cookie sheets: Arrange the rolls at least 3 inches apart on the prepared cookie sheet and flatten each to form rectangles that are 3/4 inch thick, about 3 inches wide and 7 inches long.
9. Bake first batch: Bake for 22 to 28 minutes or until the rectangles are light golden brown and firm to the touch. Remove from oven and let cool on wire racks for 5 minutes.
10. Clean cookie sheet and prepare second baking session: Wipe the cookie sheet clean.
11. Cut into slices: Using a serrated knife, cut each rectangle into half-inch slices with the cut side up on the cookie sheet.
12. Bake second batch: Place the sliced rectangles back in the oven and bake for 6 to 8 minutes or until the top surface is slightly dry.
13. Turn cookies over: Carefully turn the cookies over onto the wire racks.
14. Final baking: Continue baking for another 6 to 8 minutes or until the top surfaces are lightly crisp.
15. Cool completely: Remove from oven and let cool completely on wire racks.
16. Store: Tightly cover and store in an airtight container. Makes about 4 dozen cookies.
